title: trichera kitaibelii subsp. tomentella
intro: Trichera kitaibelii subsp. tomentella, also known as Hungarian chamomile, is a subspecies of the Trichera kitaibelii plant, a member of the asteraceae family and a flowering plant native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which have been used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Trichera kitaibelii subsp. tomentella include reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, as well as relieving menstrual cramps, insomnia, and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of the plant are often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and it is also sometimes used topically as an oil or cream to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
